---
title: "STAT 306 Project Code"
format: html
editor: visual
---

#Introduction 
##Read Data

```{r}
insurance <- read.csv("insurance.csv")
summary(insurance)
```

#Analysis 
##EDA 
```{r} 
library(ggplot2)


# Plot 1: Distribution of charges (histogram)
p1 <- ggplot(insurance, aes(x = charges)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Insurance Charges",
       x = "Charges (USD)", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()

ggsave("plot1_charges_distribution.png", plot = p1, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300)

# Plot 2: Age vs charges, colored by smoker status
p2 <- ggplot(insurance, aes(x = age, y = charges, color = smoker)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("no" = "steelblue", "yes" = "tomato")) +
  labs(title = "Age vs. Insurance Charges by Smoking Status",
       x = "Age (years)", y = "Charges (USD)", color = "Smoker") +
  theme_minimal()

ggsave("plot2_age_vs_charges.png", plot = p2, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300)

# Plot 3: BMI vs charges, colored by smoker status
p3 <- ggplot(insurance, aes(x = bmi, y = charges, color = smoker)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("no" = "steelblue", "yes" = "tomato")) +
  labs(title = "BMI vs. Insurance Charges by Smoking Status",
       x = "BMI (kg/m²)", y = "Charges (USD)", color = "Smoker") +
  theme_minimal()

ggsave("plot3_bmi_vs_charges.png", plot = p3, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```
